title: Best Custom Apparel for Breweries & Taprooms: Merch That Sells
metaDescription: Best custom apparel for craft breweries and taprooms: soft merch tees, caps, and hoodies fans actually buy. PNW decorator since 1977. Get a quote.
bodyMarkdown:
Good brewery merch does two jobs at once: it puts your brand on someone's chest around town, and it sells at the bar for real margin. Here in the Pacific Northwest, where a great taproom tee is practically part of the craft-beer uniform, the shirt behind the register can be as memorable as the flagship IPA. The trick is picking blanks people actually want to wear, then decorating your art so the colors pop.

At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've been decorating apparel since 1977, and we work with local breweries on both sides of the counter: retail merch fans take home, and staff apparel your crew wears every shift. Here's what we'd put on your merch wall.

**Retail merch: tees & caps that sell at the bar**

Your merch shelf lives and dies on soft, retail-quality blanks. A stiff, boxy shirt sits in the bin; a soft ringspun tee walks out the door. For colorful can-art logos and detailed illustrations, we lean on DTG (direct-to-garment) for full-color, photo-level detail with no color limits, and screen print when you want punchy, durable ink on a bigger run. Both make your artwork the star.

- **[Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001)** — The merch tee that sells. Soft, retail-fit, and a favorite for craft-beer drops; the go-to when the shirt itself needs to feel worth buying.
- **[District DT6000](/product.html?style=DT6000)** — The Very Important Tee. A soft, slightly heavier alt to the BC3001 with a lived-in feel — great as a second option or a limited-run color.
- **[Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112)** — The trucker cap fans reach for. Structured front, mesh back, and an ideal canvas for an embroidered logo or a printed patch.
- **[New Era NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000)** — A premium fitted cap for taprooms that want a step-up hat with a cleaner, streetwear-friendly profile.

Browse the full lines on our [Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas), [District](/custom-district), [Richardson](/custom-richardson), and [New Era](/custom-new-era) pages to spot colors that match your brand.

**Hoodies & fleece: cold-weather bestsellers**

In the PNW, a hoodie is a nine-month season. A well-decorated fleece is often the highest-ticket item on the merch wall — and the one regulars come back for.

- **[Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H)** — A value pullover hoodie that's easy to stock for a merch shelf and holds a big front print well. Your workhorse fleece.
- **[Carhartt CT100617](/product.html?style=CT100617)** — A premium, rugged hoodie for taprooms that want a heavyweight, built-to-last piece customers treat like a keeper.

See more options on our [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) and [Carhartt](/custom-carhartt) landing pages.

**Staff apparel: your crew as walking signage**

Every bartender and brewer in branded gear is free advertising — and it makes your taproom look dialed-in. Keep staff apparel consistent with your retail line so the whole room reads as one brand.

- **[Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001)** — Doubles as staff tees so your team matches the merch on the wall.
- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — An easy-care polo for managers, tour guides, or a more finished front-of-house look.
- **[Port & Company PC90H](/product.html?style=PC90H)** — A warm layer for cellar work and patio shifts when the temperature drops.

**Let's build your merch line**

Whether it's a first tee drop or a full seasonal refresh across tees, caps, and hoodies, we'll help you pick blanks that sell and decorate your art so it looks as good as the beer tastes. As a local PNW shop, we're happy to talk color, placement, and run sizes.

Start with our [quote request page](/pages/request-a-quote.html), or jump straight into the [DTG quote builder](/quote-builders/) to price a full-color merch run. Cheers to apparel that moves off the shelf.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Restaurants & Cafes: Front-of-House Picks
metaDescription: Sharp, easy-care branded apparel for restaurant and cafe staff — polos, tees, and caps that hold up all shift. PNW picks from a Milton, WA decorator.
bodyMarkdown:
Your front-of-house team is the face of your restaurant or cafe. Branded apparel that looks sharp — and stays looking sharp through a double shift — does two jobs at once: it makes your staff easy to spot and it puts your logo in front of every guest. The trick is picking garments that survive spills, constant washing, and long hours on the floor without going limp or faded.

We've been decorating apparel here in Milton, WA since 1977, and we outfit a lot of local Puget Sound restaurants, coffee shops, breweries, and food trucks. Here's what we recommend for a crew that looks put-together every service.

## Staff Polos: The Front-of-House Workhorse

A branded polo is the default for good reason — it reads as professional, it's comfortable, and it takes an embroidered logo cleanly. For restaurants and cafes, the two things that matter most are easy care and how the fabric looks after fifty washes. Pick a polo that resists wrinkles and shrugs off the inevitable coffee splash.

- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — Our go-to easy-care staff polo. Snag-resistant, holds its shape, and comes in a deep color range so it's easy to match your brand. Great all-rounder for servers, hosts, and counter staff.
- **[Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650)** — A moisture-wicking performance polo for busy floors and hot pass-throughs. Breathable, quick-drying, and it keeps staff comfortable when the dinner rush hits.

Both take embroidery beautifully, which is the most durable way to brand a polo. Browse more options on the [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) and [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) pages.

## Staff & Merch Tees: Casual, On-Brand, and Sellable

Tees fit the more relaxed vibe of a cafe, brewery, taproom, or fast-casual spot — and a good-looking tee does double duty as merch you can sell at the register. That means picking a shirt your team is happy to wear and your guests actually want to buy.

- **[Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61)** — A durable, budget-friendly core tee that's perfect for uniform tees you'll buy in volume and reorder often. Prints and holds up wash after wash.
- **[Bella+Canvas BC3001](/product.html?style=BC3001)** — The soft, retail-quality tee. This is the one to reach for when you want merch guests will pay for, or a staff tee that feels like something people choose off a shelf.

Pair a value tee for daily staff wear with a soft retail tee for merch and limited runs. See the full lineup on the [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) and [Bella+Canvas](/custom-bella-canvas) pages.

## Caps: Practical and Highly Visible

Caps are a food-service staple — they keep hair back, cut the glare, and give your logo a spot right at eye level. They're also one of the highest-value branding surfaces you can put on a team.

- **[Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112)** — The industry-standard trucker cap. Structured front for a crisp embroidered logo, mesh back for all-day breathability, and a look that works equally well on a barista or a brewery taproom crew.

More cap options live on the [Richardson](/custom-richardson) page.

## Put It All Together

A simple, effective front-of-house program usually looks like this: an easy-care polo for servers and hosts, a durable tee for kitchen-adjacent and casual roles, a soft retail tee for merch, and caps across the board — all tied together with consistent embroidery or printing of your logo. Keep colors in your brand family and your whole team reads as one crew from the door.

Ready to outfit your team? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll help you pick the right styles, colors, and decoration for your restaurant or cafe. Local to the Puget Sound? Even better — we're right here in Milton, WA and happy to talk through your program.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Real Estate & Professional Services
metaDescription: Branded polos, soft shells, and closing gifts for realtors and professional-services teams. Custom apparel from Milton, WA since 1977.
bodyMarkdown:
In real estate and professional services, your apparel is part of your credibility. Clients read a put-together, consistently branded team as an established one, and that impression starts before you say a word. Whether you're walking a listing, sitting across a closing table, or manning a booth at a community event, the goal is the same: look sharp, look consistent, and put your brand where people notice it.

At Northwest Custom Apparel in Milton, WA, we've decorated branded wear for Puget Sound brokerages, insurance offices, law firms, and consulting teams since 1977. Here's how we'd build a wardrobe that photographs well and holds up to real workweeks.

## Premium Polos That Look the Part

The embroidered polo is the workhorse of client-facing service teams. It reads more polished than a tee, works under a blazer or on its own, and gives your logo a clean home on the left chest. For a professional-services look, lean premium — the fabric and fit do a lot of the talking.

- **[TravisMathew TM1MU411](/product.html?style=TM1MU411)** — Our top pick for agents who want to look established. A premium, retail-grade polo with a refined drape that flatters at open houses and closings.
- **[OGIO OG101](/product.html?style=OG101)** — A performance polo that resists wrinkles and moves with you through a full day of showings. Great when you're in and out of the car all afternoon.
- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — An easy-care classic for larger offices that need a dependable, everyday staple in every size.

Explore the full lineup on our [TravisMathew](/custom-travismathew), [OGIO](/custom-ogio), and [Port Authority](/custom-port-and-company) brand pages.

## Layers for Showings, Site Visits & PNW Weather

Real estate happens outside, and in the Pacific Northwest that means rain, wind, and cool mornings for much of the year. A branded layer keeps your team looking coordinated in every listing photo and every client walk-through — and it's the piece people actually keep and re-wear.

- **[Eddie Bauer EB532](/product.html?style=EB532)** — A soft shell that layers over a polo for a polished, weather-ready look. Structured enough to read professional, not sporty.
- **[The North Face NF0A3LH4](/product.html?style=NF0A3LH4)** — A recognizable fleece that signals quality the moment a client sees it. Ideal for winter showings and team photos.
- **[Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650)** — A moisture-wicking polo that works as an added layer when your team wants a crisp option under a jacket.

See more on our [Eddie Bauer](/custom-eddie-bauer), [The North Face](/custom-north-face), and [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) pages.

## Closing Gifts & Client-Facing Extras

Branded apparel isn't only for your team — it's one of the most useful closing and referral gifts in the business. A quality embroidered piece keeps your name in a client's closet long after the sale, and a branded bag travels everywhere.

- **[OGIO 411092](/product.html?style=411092)** — A branded backpack that makes a genuinely useful closing gift and quietly markets you at every coffee shop and airport.
- **[TravisMathew TM1MU411](/product.html?style=TM1MU411)** — Doubles as a premium client or referral gift when you add their name or a subtle co-brand.

For field roles like property inspectors or on-site project managers who want a high-visibility look, our [custom safety apparel program](/custom-safety-apparel) offers printed hi-vis tees and hoodies that keep your branding clean. Note that printed hi-vis garments are highly visible but are not ANSI-certified safety wear — where certification is required, we can point you to certified hi-vis vests to layer over your branded apparel.

## Get Your Team Looking Established

The trick with professional-services apparel is consistency: one logo treatment, a tight color palette, and pieces that fit everyone from your newest agent to your broker of record. We'll help you standardize a program, keep reorders identical, and turn quotes around fast.

Ready to outfit your team or design a memorable closing gift? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) or start a [custom embroidery quote](/quote-builders/) and we'll get you a price right away. Family-owned, Milton-based, and printing and embroidering for PNW businesses since 1977.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Nonprofits, Events & Volunteers
metaDescription: Bulk-friendly custom apparel picks for nonprofits, 5Ks, fundraisers, and volunteer crews. Affordable tees, hoodies, and caps that unify a group.
bodyMarkdown:
Nonprofit work runs on a tight budget and a big heart. Whether you're rallying volunteers for a park cleanup, handing out shirts at a 5K start line, or dressing a gala team, you need apparel that does three things at once: stretches the budget, ships in bulk, and makes everyone look like they belong to the same cause. Here in Milton, WA, we've outfitted more community runs, food drives, and fundraiser crews than we can count since 1977 — so here's how we'd steer you.

## Start with the Right Tee

For most nonprofit events, the T-shirt is the workhorse. It's the thing volunteers wear, the thing runners keep, and the thing that shows up in every group photo. When you're ordering by the dozen (or the hundred), the savings per shirt add up fast — so the goal is a comfortable, print-friendly blank that looks unified across a crowd.

- **[Gildan 2000 (Ultra Cotton)](/product.html?style=2000)** — Our go-to for big volunteer groups. Durable, budget-friendly, and available in a huge color range, so it's easy to match your brand or theme. Best value when you're ordering in real volume.
- **[Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61)** and **[PC54](/product.html?style=PC54)** — Reliable core value tees that print clean and hold up wash after wash. Great "safe choice" when you need a lot of shirts and zero surprises.
- **[District DT6000 (Very Important Tee)](/product.html?style=DT6000)** — A softer, more retail-feeling step up for donor gifts, staff shirts, or gala volunteers you want to feel a little more premium — without leaving the budget lane entirely.

Not sure which fits your run? Browse the full **[Gildan](/custom-gildan)**, **[Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company)**, and **[District](/custom-district)** lineups to compare.

## Layers and Caps to Round Out the Crew

Cool-morning cleanups, outdoor markets, and PNW fall events call for a layer. And a cap is the easiest way to make a volunteer team read as "official" from across a parking lot.

- **[Port & Company PC90H (fleece hoodie)](/product.html?style=PC90H)** — A value hoodie that keeps volunteers warm at early call times and doubles as a thank-you gift. Comfortable, affordable, and easy to decorate. See more in **[Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company)**.
- **[Richardson 112 (trucker cap)](/product.html?style=112)** — The classic staff and volunteer cap. Adjustable snapback fits everyone, so you don't fight with sizes, and an embroidered logo up front instantly identifies your team. More options on the **[Richardson](/custom-richardson)** page.

Pairing a matching tee and cap is the fastest way to make a small crew look organized and trustworthy — which matters when volunteers are the face of your cause.

## Why Screen Printing Wins for Big Runs

For large, single-design orders — think a few hundred shirts for a race or a walk — screen printing is almost always the smart call. Once your artwork is set up, printing more shirts gets more efficient by the piece, which is exactly what you want when quantities climb. The result is a bold, long-lasting print that looks consistent across the entire batch, so every shirt in that finish-line photo matches.

If you've got a smaller crew or multi-color, photo-style artwork, we'll walk you through DTG or DTF options instead. Either way, the recommendation is honest and matched to your run size — not a one-size-fits-all upsell.

## Let's Get Your Group Outfitted

Tell us your event, your headcount, and your timeline, and we'll help you land on the right blank and the right decoration method to stretch every dollar. Get a fast, no-pressure quote through our **[screen-print quote builder](/quote-builders/)** or **[request a quote here](/pages/request-a-quote.html)** — and let's make your next event look like one team.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Landscaping & Lawn Care Crews
metaDescription: Durable, high-visibility custom workwear for landscaping and lawn care crews — tough tees, caps, hoodies, and hi-vis gear. Get a quote from NWCA.
bodyMarkdown:
Landscaping and lawn care crews put clothes through the wringer. Sun, sweat, mud, thorns, string-trimmer debris, early-morning chill, and afternoon heat — all in a single day. Your gear has to survive the season, keep your crew visible near roadsides, and turn every yard you work into a rolling billboard for your business. Here in Milton, WA, we outfit plenty of Puget Sound crews, and the right mix comes down to three things: durability, visibility, and a clean logo people remember.

Below is what we recommend for outdoor crews, and why each piece earns its spot on the truck.

## High-Visibility Gear (Get This Right)

Visibility matters most when your crew works near curbs, streets, and parking lots. A quick compliance note worth understanding up front: a printed hi-vis tee is highly visible and great for brand presence, but only an actual **ANSI-certified vest** meets roadside safety-garment standards. If your crew works roadside or your contracts require certified gear, put them in vests — then use branded tees and caps for everything else.

- **[CornerStone CSV105](/product.html?style=CSV105)** — an ANSI-certified hi-vis vest. This is your compliant, roadside-ready layer that goes right over any shirt.
- **Screen-printed safety-stripe tees** — our [custom safety-stripe program](/custom-safety-apparel) puts bright, high-visibility striping and your logo on comfortable tees. Excellent visibility and branding for general yard work (just remember these are not a substitute for a certified vest where one is required).

Between the two, most crews carry certified [CornerStone](/custom-cornerstone) vests for roadside jobs and wear branded hi-vis tees the rest of the time.

## Everyday Workwear That Survives the Season

This is the daily uniform — the stuff that gets worn, washed, and abused all week. Go with proven, tough fabrics and a logo decoration that holds up.

- **[Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61)** — a value crew tee that takes screen printing beautifully and holds up wash after wash. Order these in bulk so nobody's ever short a clean shirt. See more from [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company).
- **[Carhartt CT100617](/product.html?style=CT100617)** — a genuinely tough hooded sweatshirt for cold mornings, fall cleanups, and winter work. Carhartt durability your crew already trusts. Browse [custom Carhartt](/custom-carhartt).
- **[Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112)** — the trucker cap crews actually want to wear. Structured front for a crisp embroidered logo, mesh back for all-day breathability. This is the piece your customers see most. More [custom Richardson](/custom-richardson) caps here.

A good rule of thumb: screen print your bulk tees to keep the per-shirt cost down on big runs, and embroider your caps and premium pieces for a sharp, long-lasting look.

## Build a Simple, Season-Ready Program

You don't need a huge catalog — you need a tight kit that covers every condition and keeps your crew looking like a team:

- **Warm-weather core:** branded PC61 tees plus Richardson 112 caps.
- **Visibility layer:** CornerStone CSV105 vests for roadside work, safety-stripe tees for everything else.
- **Cold-weather layer:** Carhartt CT100617 hoodies for spring and fall mornings.

Stick with one logo treatment and one or two shirt colors so new hires are easy to outfit and reorders stay simple. When you're ready to scale, we can hold your artwork on file and run predictable seasonal reorders so you're never scrambling before the spring rush.

## Get Your Crew Outfitted

We've been decorating apparel in Milton, WA since 1977 — in-house embroidery, screen print, DTG, and DTF, all under one roof. Tell us your crew size, your colors, and whether you need certified hi-vis, and we'll help you build a durable, visible, on-brand kit.

[Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) and we'll get your landscaping crew set for the season.

title: Best Custom Apparel for Auto Shops, Mechanics & Dealerships
metaDescription: The best custom work tees, hoodies, caps, and polos for auto shops, mechanics, and dealerships. Rugged, professional branded workwear from Milton, WA.
bodyMarkdown:
Branded apparel does two jobs at once in the automotive world. In the bay, it has to survive grease, brake dust, and constant movement. On the lot, it has to look sharp enough to close a sale. Those are two different wardrobes, and the shops that get uniforms right usually split the difference by role.

We've been outfitting Puget Sound businesses from our shop in Milton, WA since 1977, and auto shops are one of our steadiest customers. Here's how we'd build out a program for a service department, a dealership, or an independent garage.

## For the techs in the bay: durable and easy to replace

Bay techs go through shirts. That's just the reality of the job, so the goal is a tough, comfortable garment you can reorder in bulk without thinking twice. Cotton and cotton-blend tees take a clean embroidered logo or a bold screen print, and heavier fleece keeps techs warm in an unheated bay through a PNW winter.

- **[Port & Company PC61](/product.html?style=PC61)** — Our workhorse everyday work tee. Heavyweight cotton, holds up to repeated washing, and comes in a deep color range so every tech matches.
- **[Port & Company PC54](/product.html?style=PC54)** — A slightly softer core tee with the same durability. Great when you want a more comfortable feel without giving up toughness.
- **[Carhartt CT100617](/product.html?style=CT100617)** — A genuinely tough hooded sweatshirt built for cold mornings and rough handling. The Carhartt name also signals quality to your crew and customers alike.
- **[Carhartt CTK121](/product.html?style=CTK121)** — A rugged crewneck sweatshirt for guys who don't want a hood snagging under a lift or in an engine bay.

Cotton and heavy fleece are the right call here. They embroider and print cleanly, and they take abuse. Browse the full lineup on our [Carhartt](/custom-carhartt) and [Port & Company](/custom-port-and-company) landing pages.

## For service writers and sales on the lot: professional and polished

The folks greeting customers and walking the lot need to look put-together. A clean embroidered polo reads as professional and photographs well next to your inventory. Performance fabrics matter here too — they resist wrinkles and shrug off a warm afternoon on the asphalt.

- **[Sport-Tek ST650](/product.html?style=ST650)** — A moisture-wicking performance polo that stays crisp through a full shift. Our top pick for service writers and sales staff.
- **[Port Authority K500](/product.html?style=K500)** — An easy-care polo with a more traditional feel, great for managers and front-desk staff.

For a step up in look and feel, our [Sport-Tek](/custom-sport-tek) and [Port Authority](/custom-port-authority) pages have the full range, and we can help you match colors to your brand.

## Caps finish the uniform

A branded cap is the single most-worn piece in most shops, and it keeps your logo visible on every customer interaction. Techs, writers, and owners all wear them.

- **[Richardson 112](/product.html?style=112)** — The classic trucker cap. Structured front for a clean embroidered logo, mesh back for all-day comfort. The default choice for a reason.
- **[New Era NE1000](/product.html?style=NE1000)** — A premium fitted cap when you want a more modern, upscale look for sales staff or a limited owner's edition.

See color and style options on our [Richardson](/custom-richardson) and [New Era](/custom-new-era) pages.

## Pull it together

A smart auto-shop program usually looks like this: durable embroidered or screen-printed tees and Carhartt fleece for the bay, performance polos for the lot, and a shared cap that ties the whole crew together. Roll a name or department under the logo and you've got a uniform that works for a two-bay independent or a full dealership.

We handle embroidery, screen print, DTG, and DTF all in-house in Milton, so we can keep your reorders consistent and quick. Ready to spec out your shop's gear? [Request a quote](/pages/request-a-quote.html) or jump straight into our [embroidery quote builder](/quote-builders/) and we'll help you build a program that fits how your team actually works.
